issue_title: Multiline string parsing is wrong
issue_body:
I believe this line in _load_value:

```
        testv = v[1:].split('"')
```

is causing problems when there is a multiline string with quote marks in it. E.g. this simple string causes toml.loads to error with `toml.TomlDecodeError: Stuff after closed string. WTF?`:

```
value = """
this "string" should work
"""
```

Here is my test case:

```
import toml
text = '''value = """\nthis "string" should work\n"""\n'''
parsed_toml = toml.loads(text)
```

I looked in the toml validation suite you mentioned that you were using, and I noticed it didn't have test cases covering multiline strings that would catch this.

If I figure out a fix that doesn't change too much code, I'll send it to you.

issue_title: buffer.py UTF-8 Decoding error
issue_body:
Hey,

got another stacktrace:

```
#!python

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "./ratbot.py", line 293, in <module>
    main()
  File "./ratbot.py", line 290, in main
    bot.start()
  File "/home/luke/pipsqueak2-deploy/lib/python3.4/site-packages/irc/bot.py", line 265, in start
    super(SingleServerIRCBot, self).start()
  File "/home/luke/pipsqueak2-deploy/lib/python3.4/site-packages/irc/client.py", line 1246, in start
    self.reactor.process_forever()
  File "/home/luke/pipsqueak2-deploy/lib/python3.4/site-packages/irc/client.py", line 278, in process_forever
    self.process_once(timeout)
  File "/home/luke/pipsqueak2-deploy/lib/python3.4/site-packages/irc/client.py", line 259, in process_once
    self.process_data(i)
  File "/home/luke/pipsqueak2-deploy/lib/python3.4/site-packages/irc/client.py", line 216, in process_data
    c.process_data()
  File "/home/luke/pipsqueak2-deploy/lib/python3.4/site-packages/irc/client.py", line 579, in process_data
    for line in self.buffer:
  File "/home/luke/pipsqueak2-deploy/lib/python3.4/site-packages/irc/buffer.py", line 102, in lines
    self.handle_exception()
  File "/home/luke/pipsqueak2-deploy/lib/python3.4/site-packages/irc/buffer.py", line 100, in lines
    yield line.decode(self.encoding, self.errors)
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0xfc in position 109: invalid start byte
```

Doesn't seem like there is any way for application layer (library consumer) to get ahold of this decoding error and handle it. Imho LenientLineDecodingBuffer should be used by default, or DecodingBuffer class to use should be made configurable. On IRC clients will send any kind of data, and a client shouldn't explode because of non-utf8 data sent.

issue_title: Entry points scripts are not isolated
issue_body:
After investigating on strange issues on my module, it seems entry points scripts (defined in packages setup.py) can be called in a different virtualenv than the active one.

Let's say there is a package `hello` with entry point `sayHi`

In env A, package is not installed.
In env B, package is installed. 

The following steps should reproduce the problem (it seems related to switching virtual envs)

```
pew workon B
pip install hello
pew workon A
sayHi
```
sayHi is effectively called.

With env A active, doing the following
```
python
import distutils.spawn
distutils.spawn.find_executable('hello')
```
will return the path to the hello package in env B

issue_title: Stop pypcap from capturing and writing network traffic to a file
issue_body:
I'm capturing network traffic with pypcap and writing it to a file with dpkt. Problem is I can't delete the file as I get an error saying it's still in use by another process. 

    seconds = 15
    starttime = datetime.datetime.now()

    pc = pcap.pcap()
    file = open('file.pcap', 'wb')
    pcapfile = dpkt.pcap.Writer(file)

    for timestamp, packet in pc:
        currenttime = datetime.datetime.now()
        timedelta = currenttime - starttime
        if timedelta.seconds > seconds:
            break

        pcapfile.writepkt(packet,timestamp)
    pcapfile.close()
    file.close()

    time.sleep(10)

    os.remove(file.pcap) = file in use by another process error


I'm currently overwriting the pcap file the next time I capture traffic. Doing this overwrites the file data most of the time, but occasionally it doesn't work and I end up with a large pcap file encompassing two sets of network capture data.  

How do you close the pcapy/dpkt process handle?

Also does anyone know a better way to stop pcapy capturing traffic after 15 seconds?

Thanks

issue_title: dpkt.http needs to check for '\r\n', since we may have fragmented requests

Sometimes HTTP requests are too large to fit in one TCP segment, which means on parsing pcap TCP segments it is possible to pass an incomplete header to the dpkt.http decoder. Fixed with the following patch in http.py:




[patch.txt](https://github.com/kbandla/dpkt/files/230908/patch.txt)

issue_title: Table of Contents perspective no longer working?
Implement Transfomer.phaser()
Is StrictRedis retry behaviour as expected?
issue_body:
## Checklist

- Python version
   3.6
- Using hiredis or just Python parser
   Python parser
- Using uvloop or just asyncio event loop
   Asyncio
- Does issue exists against the `master` branch of aredis?
   Yes

## Steps to reproduce
https://github.com/NoneGG/aredis/blob/395ce420a5b477dfb51aa632c15912a1111f40ea/aredis/client.py#L156-L161
During debugging another issue, I find that the logic here is a bit strange.
The senario:
2 different types of errors ConnectionError and TimeoutError will be caught here, and from the following if clause, isinstance(TimoutError) is added as a condition, so I am assuming that the expected behaviour for retry is:
1. The user sets the retry_on_timeout to True.
2. It's a TimeoutError.

## Expected behavior
The expect behaviour can be interpretaed with a truth table(T means do retry, F means not):

|      TimeoutError\retry | true | false | 
|-------|------|:-----:|
| true  | T    |   F   |
| false | F    |   F   |
## Actual behavior
The actual behaviour can be interpretaed with a truth table(T means do retry, F means not):

|      TimeoutError\retry | true | false | 
|-------|------|:-----:|
| true  | T    |   F   |
| false | T    |   T   |

## Conclusion
If my understanding of the expected behaviour is correct, then we need to fix the problem of retrying on encountering connection error.(I already created a PR for it)

Here is a script for validating the current behaviour and behavour after the fix:
```python
# script for validating the current and fixed behaviour truth table

do_retry =          [True, True, False, False]
is_timeout_error =  [False,True, True,  False]
zipped = list(zip(do_retry, is_timeout_error))

# current behaviour:
print("----------------------------------------")
print("Current behaviour: ")
print("----------------------------------------")
for do_retry, is_timeout_error in zipped:
    print("Condition: retry-{}, timeout-{}".format(do_retry, is_timeout_error))
    if not do_retry and is_timeout_error:
        print("Result: not retry")
    else:
        print("Result: retry")

# behaviour after fix:
print("----------------------------------------")
print("Fixed behaviour: ")
print("----------------------------------------")
for do_retry, is_timeout_error in zipped:
    print("Condition: retry: {}, timeout: {}".format(do_retry, is_timeout_error))
    if not (do_retry and is_timeout_error):
        print("Result: not retry")
    else:
        print("Result: retry")
print("----------------------------------------")
```
Script output:

```
----------------------------------------
Current behaviour:
----------------------------------------
Condition: retry-True, timeout-False
Result: retry
Condition: retry-True, timeout-True
Result: retry
Condition: retry-False, timeout-True
Result: not retry
Condition: retry-False, timeout-False
Result: retry
----------------------------------------
Fixed behaviour:
----------------------------------------
Condition: retry: True, timeout: False
Result: not retry
Condition: retry: True, timeout: True
Result: retry
Condition: retry: False, timeout: True
Result: not retry
Condition: retry: False, timeout: False
Result: not retry
----------------------------------------
```

issue_title: Searching/requesting Organizations via external_id
issue_body:
According to the Zendesk docs it should be possible to [search for an organiation via an associated external_id](https://developer.zendesk.com/rest_api/docs/core/organizations#search-organizations-by-external-id). 

Trying this using zenpy returns no results, though an organization with the matching id exists.

    zenpy_client.search(type='organization', external_id=organization_id)

Looking at the Zendesk docs the endpoint given is an organization-specific search, i.e.

    GET /api/v2/organizations/search.json?external_id={external_id}

Is it possible to make a request against this endpoint using zenpy? Or is the problem somewhere else? A search for users using the external_id works fine.

issue_title: SearchEndpoint cannot handle args with '+' character
issue_body:
I was testing the Zenpy client today with a gmail address that has a '+' character in it, and I couldn't figure out why it was returning no results. After some digging, I found this:

```python
from zenpy.lib import endpoint
e = endpoint.EndpointFactory('search')
print e('mfwmtesting+201803140@gmail.com', type='ticket')
# ==> 'search.json?query=mfwmtesting+201803140@gmail.com+type:"ticket"'
```

It seems to me that the issue is that the _args_ are not getting properly URL encoded, and the "+" in my email address is being interpreted as a space character in the query parameter. Furthermore, the [ZD API reference](https://developer.zendesk.com/rest_api/docs/core/search#url-encoding-the-search-string) states that the entire search string should be URL encoded. It appears that the code is trying to do that, however it seems to miss encoding (at least) the _args_. 

**ZenPy Version:** 1.2.6

To give a bit of background on how Blender in Python works, Blender is compiled into a `bpy.pyd` file imported through `import bpy` whereby a headless Blender process will be spun up and the root python object is accessible in the current python environment. I'm only posting this because while it doesn't work in `green`, I just tried and it does work in `pytest` (see below). issue_title: conn.modify documentation issue

It looks like there is a documentation error on this page:
http://ldap3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/connections.html

Specifically, the changes structure for the modify operation is listed as:

This does not work, giving `ldap3.core.exceptions.LDAPChangesError: malformed change`. Looking at the test code, it should be:

    {‘attribute1’: (operation, [val1, val2, ...]), ‘attribute2’: (operation, [val1, val2, ...])}

Thanks!
(There operation Tuple should not be enclosed in a list.)

issue_title: Comparable/hashable (d)rectangles in Python
issue_body:
As of now, `dlib.drectangle` and `dlib.rectangle` instances are not comparable (nor hash-stable):

```python
>>> import dlib
>>> x1 = dlib.drectangle(0, 1, 2, 3)
>>> x2 = dlib.drectangle(0, 1, 2, 3)
>>> assert x1 == x2, 'Rectangles differ'
AssertionError: Rectangle differ
>>> assert hash(x1) == hash(x2), 'Hashes differ'
AssertionError: Hashes differ
```

Therefore, they cannot be used as dictionary keys for instance, nor in a set.
Solving this issue can be solved by implementing `__hash__` and `__eq__` methods in Python API.

I am willing to help but am not familiar with interfacing C and Python API so I don't really know where to start...

issue_title: Keyring package doesn't work if we use it from Windows service build using Python
issue_body:
I have came across a bug on "import keyring" when called from Windows Service. This was happening due to `username` default parameter in `keyring.getpassbackend.get_password()`. get_default_user() was raising exception because Windows service does not run as a logged-in user.

Following patch is required in `keyring.getpassbackend.py`
    
    #!python
    
    11c11,13
    <                            username=get_default_user()):
    ---
    >                            username=None):
    >     if username is None:
    >         username = get_default_user()
    

Please reassign this bug to responsible developer.

Thanks.

----------------------------------------

- Originally reported by: Vijayendra Bapte
- Originally created at: 2011-03-25T11:16:46.157

issue_title: reading combination of columns from an extension
issue_body:
Is there already the chance to read not just a column from a Binary Table, but a combination of columns?

for example:
```
hdu = FITS('file')
events = hdu['EVENTS']
expression = 'log10(col1) * col2'
events[expression].read()
```

The reason why I want to do this is that I only know `expression` at run time (via a configuration file).

It seems the `CFITSIO` can do this via the `readKey()` function.

issue_title: Issue with HP Comware with banners that require Enter to accept
issue_body:
Hello,

I noticed an issue with version 1.3 and 1.4 of Netmiko that was not a problem with version 1.2.8 of the package.

My HP comware switches have a disclaimer that requires the user to press the enter or y key to accept.  When setting up a ConnectHandler object, the process hangs.  This does not happen on my HP switches that do not have a disclaimer.   I have traced the hanging to the session_preparation() function in the hp_comware_ssh.py file.  Between versions 1.2.8 and 1.3 the function was changed from:

def session_preparation(self):
        """
        Prepare the session after the connection has been established.
        Extra time to read HP banners.
        """
        delay_factor = self.select_delay_factor(delay_factor=0)
        time.sleep(2 * delay_factor)
        self.clear_buffer()
        self.set_base_prompt()
        self.disable_paging(command="\nscreen-length disable\n")

to

def session_preparation(self):
        """
        Prepare the session after the connection has been established.
        Extra time to read HP banners.
        """
        self._test_channel_read(pattern=r'[>\]]')
        self.set_base_prompt()
        self.disable_paging(command="\nscreen-length disable\n")

Thanks,
Bob

issue_title: Auto discovery enhancement
issue_body:
What do you think about enchaning the auto discovery module created in the beginning of 2017 by using SSH discovery instead of SNMP.

The issue I see in using SNMP is that we need to use an extra protocol and extra credentials to perform auto-discovery. This means that auto-discovery only works if you have SNMP enabled on the asset.

I was thinking about doing auto-discovery over a preliminary SSH connection, this way:

1. Try to connect over SSH port using paramiko
2. Use SSH authentication with provided credentials (or maybe application authentication as needed by Cisco WLC)
3. Execute several "discovery commands" (i.e '*show version | inc IOS*' on Cisco IOS or '*show version | inc Nexus*' or Cisco NX-OS, etc). The idea is that the command's output does not span longer than the default terminal length.
4. Close the SSH connection
5. Based on the output string, return the corresponding driver to be used with the **ConnectHandler** class.

This way we are only using one unique set of credential for both discovery and real SSH connection for interacting with the asset.

Do you think it's worth working on it ?

issue_title: Using str in some of the netmiko code
issue_body: Shouldn't be doing this for py2/py3 compatibility.

issue_title: Consider using py.test
issue_body:
I believe the test-suite could be made more clear and shrink a little if using ``py.test``. In any case, I prefer the py.test style and it saves an indent-level.

So, tests would look like:

```python
def test_something(fixture0, fixture1):
    assert False, "It failed"
```

Anything in a ``setUp`` method would be converted to an ``@pytest.fixture`` and in some cases paramtrization could be used to good effect.

(This is al low-priority thing, but anyone interested in Twisted, py.test and Tor might like to tackle it).

issue_body:
See https://forum.m-siemens.de/d/23-table-name-from-the-table-object

My main concern is how to add this in a backwards-compatible way, as I'm not totally sure what guarantees we specify in the docs about what arguments the table class should take.

issue_title: try to make it exciting to run - brainstorming
issue_body:
I like things that grow. I like being rewarded constantly.

Can we count and display stuff while fishnet runs? Like, sum of moves analysed, nodes searched, evaluation depth. Give me something to look at and brag about.

Also lichess could collect these stats on the server and send them to the client on start, for persistence.

issue_title: lxml.etree.XMLSyntaxError: internal error: Huge input lookup
issue_body:
I met this exception info when I was parsing a captured .pcap file.

It seems that the XML file of some packets have exceeded the 9.5Mb limit of lxml lib.
(http://making-security-measurable.1364806.n2.nabble.com/libxml2-lxml-Parsing-large-9-5mb-XML-Documents-td7580595.html)

According to the solution above, I added two new lines in "/Lib/site-packages/pyshark/tshark/tshark_xml.py" at line 26:
"
parser = lxml.objectify.makeparser(huge_tree=True)
xml_pkt = lxml.objectify.fromstring(xml_pkt, parser)
"
then the problem was solved.

issue_title: "Goto" prefers local module instead of global one
issue_body:
Given the following project setup, in a virtualenv where stripe is installed (`pip install stripe`):

```
.
├── proj
│   └── app
│       ├── __init__.py
│       ├── models.py
│       └── stripe
│           └── __init__.py
```

With `models.py`:
```
import stripe

stripe.error
```

Jedi-vim's `goto` will use the project's module instead of the global one.

It behaves as if `from . import stripe` would be used instead of `import stripe`.

Python 3.6.3, latest parso and jedi(-vim).

The same applies when moving the `__init__.py` from `app` to `proj`.

issue_title: corners_to_keypoints fails when no corners detected
issue_body:
## Overview

The `corners_to_keypoints` utility does not perform any checks on the input to ensure it is an iterable as expected.  This can cause error if using a GFTT keypoint detector on an image that doesn't have any keypoints found (ie `corners_to_keypoints(None)`).

(I ran into the issue with all black images from a VideoCapture while the camera was starting up)

## Example to reproduce error

```python
import cv2
import numpy as np
import imutils.feature.factories as kp_factory

# create blank all black 'image'
image = np.zeros(16, dtype='uint8').reshape(4, 4)

# init gftt keypoint detector using imutils factory
kp_detector = kp_factory.FeatureDetector_create('GFTT')

# detect keypoints in blank image (same defaults as `FeatureDetector_create('GFTT')`)
# result assigned to cv2_kps is `None`
cv2_kps = cv2.goodFeaturesToTrack(image, 0, 0.01, 1)  

# attempt keypoint detection with imutils (causes error below)
imutils_kps = kp_detector.detect(image)
```

### Resulting error

```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
   File "test_kp_none.py", line 12, in <module>
      imutils_kps = kp_detector.detect(image)
   File ".../imutils/imutils/feature/gftt.py", line 21, in detect
      return corners_to_keypoints(cnrs)
   File ".../imutils/imutils/feature/helpers.py", line 6, in corners_to_keypoints
      return [cv2.KeyPoint(kp[0][0], kp[0][1], 1) for kp in corners]
TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not iterable
```

## Proposed fix

A simple if else to return `None` if the input to `corners_to_keypoints` is `None`.  This fixes the error and seems to mirror the `cv2` behavior.  I've only tested with 'GFTT' created by the `imutils` factory because from it looks like thats the only place `corners_to_keypoints` is used.

```python
def corners_to_keypoints(corners):
    """function to take the corners from cv2.GoodFeaturesToTrack and return cv2.KeyPoints"""
    if corners is None:
        keypoints = None
    else:
        keypoints = [cv2.KeyPoint(kp[0][0], kp[0][1], 1) for kp in corners]

    return keypoints
```

issue_title: Feature request for a hamming circle?
issue_body:
Feature request for a function to create words of hamming distance `n` in a given alphabet from a target word?

Let me know if this looks good and I can create a PR with tests. I won't be able to write the C port however.

```python
def hamming_circle(word, n, alphabet):
    """Create all words of hamming distance `n` in a given alphabet from a
    target word.

    Examples
    ---
    >>> sorted(hamming_circle('abc', 0, 'abc'))
    ['abc']
    >>> sorted(hamming_circle('abc', 1, 'abc'))
    ['aac', 'aba', 'abb', 'acc', 'bbc', 'cbc']
    >>> sorted(hamming_circle('aaa', 2, 'ab'))
    ['abb', 'bab', 'bba']

    """
    for positions in combinations(range(len(word)), n):
        for replacements in product(range(len(alphabet)), repeat=n):
            skip = False
            cousin = list(word)

            for position, replacement in zip(positions, replacements):
                if cousin[position] == alphabet[replacement]:
                    skip = True
                else:
                    cousin[position] = alphabet[replacement]

            if not skip:
                yield ''.join(cousin)
```

issue_title: Auxiliary Module with Embedded Type Executables
issue_body:
When a custom type includes embedded `module procedures`, we have previously tested them by making the subroutine/function that the embedded method points to `public`. Then the unit test driver can just call the subroutine easily using the existing framework. This worked for lots of test cases, but one broke this morning. The `gfortran` compiler failed with `Abort trap 6` error. After some digging we found out that:

- if we compiled `fpy_auxiliary` in its own folder and then copied the `.mod` and `.o` files to the unit test staging folder, the compiler would fail.
- if we copied `fpy_auxiliary.f90` to the staging folder and compiled the unit test completely by itself, with all the source code, then the compiler would *not* fail.

Evidently, the two versions of the `fpy_auxiliary` files were different. The only difference in code between the two was the `public` statement in the unit test version of the module that made the subroutine being tested public. We adjusted the call to the subroutine manually by calling `type%subroutine` and omitting the first argument, and then recompiled using the `.mod` and `.o` versions and everything, but then we ran into an error where the compiler also wanted the auxiliary module's dependencies... So we compiled using the auxiliary shared library and then it worked.

So, to fix this problem, here are the steps:
1. Compile the auxiliary shared library just the way we have been doing it.
2. Instead of copying the `fpy_auxiliary.o` and `.mod` files, symlink to the shared library.
3. Prevent embedded methods from being made public  in `fortpy.testing.method._check_exec_public`.
4. Change the call signature for embedded methods to use the `%`-syntax and omit the first argument.
5. Execute the unit test drivers with the prefix `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH=...` so that the shared library can be located by the executable.

issue_title: An alternative to typing.ClassVar?
issue_body:
In issue #14 we added support for `typing.ClassVar` annotations. If an annotation is a `ClassVar`, then it's considered to not be a field (that is, it's not set on instances of the dataclass, it's not in `__init__`, etc.).

There's ongoing discussion on python-ideas and python-dev about dropping the typing module from the stdlib.

I'm wondering what we should do about `ClassVar` if typing is in fact dropped from the stdlib.

Currently, the code doesn't import typing. It just looks for `"typing"` in sys.modules, and if that's present, assumes it's the typing module and looks inside of it for `ClassVar`. I think this is a good approach. However, if typing is no longer part of the stdlib, I guess it's possible for another module named typing to be used in its place, and then I need to be more defensive about looking inside `sys.modules['typing']`. Is that case worth worrying about? I sort of think it's not, although it would be easy enough to add a `getattr(typing, 'ClassVar')` to the code.

The other thing to worry about is: what if typing is removed, but something in the stdlib wants to have a dataclass with a ClassVar? In https://mail.python.org/pipermail/python-dev/2017-November/150176.html, @ncoghlan suggested having dataclasses create its own ClassVar. Another option that's just as good, although the syntax is somewhat worse, is to add a param to `field()` that says "this isn't really a field". Something like:
```
@dataclass
class C:
    x: int
    classvar: field(int, not_a_field=True)
```
In either event, mypy would need to know about it, to know that `__init__` for the class would only have one parameter, `x`.

If we go with any of these approaches, I think we should still keep the code in dataclasses that understands real `typing.ClassVar` fields. That seems like the most natural way to write code, outside of the stdlib.

issue_title: Support for atomic requests
issue_body:
The `json_view()` decorator catches all exceptions which means that with [`ATOMIC_REQUESTS = True`](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/dev/ref/settings/#std:setting-DATABASE-ATOMIC_REQUESTS) exceptions do not cause a rollback.

It's easy to work around by explicitly putting `@transaction.atomic` decorator on a view below `@json_view` but it's a gotcha and also easy to forget. I think that `json_view()` should be doing the same thing Django does to get the rollback behavior of normal views (I'm not sure if it's just this decorator or a bit more magic). Or at least document the current behavior in the README.

issue_title: c.kv.put('a//b', '1') results in an empty key `a/b`
issue_body:
This is due to kennethreitz/requests#2590 : with Requests v2.x, when `PUT /v1/kv/a//b` is redirected to `PUT /v1/kv/a/b`, the content data is dropped (and Consul cannot distinguish it from a genuine empty content, returning 200.)

We can either wait for Requests v3.0 to be released or use `os.path.normpath()` to rewrite an offending key on our side. I'm not 100% sure if it's valid to rewrite `a//b` to `a/b` when we think about an abstract notion of a "key" but the current implementation of Consul handles a key as something like a path (as opposed to what's merely passed around as a path) so it's okay at least for now.

issue_title: Why does it this module not load when executing cron
Client disconnect causes SocketIO server to leave all rooms?
issue_body:
I am running into the following situation (I think):

1. Three clients, John, Mary and Sue join a room.
2. Sometime later, the John's client disconnects (e.g. browser reload or whatnot)

Now, the following happens on the flask-socketio server upon disconnection:

3. The related `GenericNamespace` receives a [`recv_disconnect()`](https://github.com/miguelgrinberg/Flask-SocketIO/blob/master/flask_socketio/__init__.py#L92) call.

4. That in turn calls [`_leave_all_rooms()`](https://github.com/miguelgrinberg/Flask-SocketIO/blob/master/flask_socketio/__init__.py#L97), which causes the server to [leave all rooms](https://github.com/miguelgrinberg/Flask-SocketIO/blob/master/flask_socketio/__init__.py#L191), .

As a result, the server no longer has a `socketio.rooms` record.  So if the server needs to send a message to the room via `socketio.emit(...)`, the message will never reach the clients (Mary and Sue) because `socketio.rooms` is now empty just because John left.

Is my understanding of this sequence correct?

issue_title: GooglePointFieldInlineWidget not working
Misleading datetime in revert message. Easy fix.
issue_body:
Reversion is a wonderful project, nevertheless I found a subtle bug.

The revert action saves the admin log message "Reverted to previous version, saved on <datetime>". When the `settings.USE_TZ = True`, then the code `localize(version.revision.date_created)` makes the timezone-aware datetime object nicely human readable, but cuts off the UTC timezone info.

I believe the fix is how Django renders the datetimes in templates: `localize(timezone.template_localtime(...)`. Thanks.

issue_title: Make test suite fail if benchmark is unsatisfactory
issue_body:
Hello,
I read the documentation and hope I did not miss something completely obvious.

My use case: I would like to use `pytest-benchmark` for continuous integration. A part of my test suite is actually performance tests, i.e. making sure that my `toto` function does not take longer than, let's say, 20ms.

I would like the test suite to *fail* is some modifications in the code make `toto()` exceeds the 20ms. I am aware of the `--benchmark-compare-fail=EXPR`, but I think what I am looking for is more specific.

I have no idea what would be the best way to describe this, perhaps:
```python
@pytest.mark.benchmark(fail_at=0.02)
def test_toto(benchmark):
    benchmark(toto)
```
Or maybe:
```python
def test_toto(benchmark):
    results = benchmark(toto, fail_at=0.02)
```
Or provide a way for the user to access the results of the benchmark? Before using `pytest-benchmark`, I would do something like this:
```python
def test_toto(benchmark):
    results = benchmark(toto)
    if results.average_time > 0.02:
        pytest.fail('Exceeding 20ms!')
```
Would this make sense in `pytest-benchmark`?

issue_title: remove_all_matching assumes it's being passed a single name but is frequently called with a list if names

For example, [here](https://github.com/jonhadfield/python-hosts/blob/7eddbb4e8bcac723a14092db87d69c94c56dd41a/python_hosts/hosts.py#L357) we call `remove_all_matching` with a list of names to match on. issue_title: Handle the case when python is loaded by ld.so
issue_body:
When python is loaded by `ld.so`, python executable would not be the actual `argv[0]` in `/proc/self/cmdline` . This can be illustrated as:

```
SPT_DEBUG=1 /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2  $(which python3) -c 'import setproctitle, os; setproctitle.setproctitle("haha"); os.system("ps -o args --pid {}".format(os.getpid())); '
```
which outputs

```
[SPT]: module init
[SPT]: reading argc/argv from Python main
[SPT]: found 3 arguments
[SPT]: walking from environ to look for the arguments
[SPT]: found environ at 0x7ffd3dbdf3da
[SPT]: found argv[2] at 0x7ffd3dbdf367: import setproctitle, os; setproctitle.setproctitle("haha"); os.system("ps -o args --pid {}".format(os.getpid())); 
[SPT]: found argv[1] at 0x7ffd3dbdf364: -c
[SPT]: argv[0] should be at 0x7ffd3dbdf353
[SPT]: found argv[0]: /usr/bin/python3
COMMAND
/lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2 haha  
``` 

I could not find a portable way to handle this, and currently only come up with a solution of adding two environment vars: `SPT_ARGC_DELTA` and `SPT_ARGV0`. In the above example, setting `SPT_ARGC_DELTA=1 SPT_ARGV0=/lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2` would produce desired output.

The patch is at https://github.com/dvarrazzo/py-setproctitle/compare/master...jia-kai:master . If you think it is adequate, I can send a PR.

issue_title: When user_load return None, session['user_id'] should be cleared
issue_body:
My custom `user_loader` queries the database for an user. Between requests, the user can be invalidated in the database (either when the column `inactive_after` has been reached, or when this column is changed). In this case, my `user_loader` returns `None`. 

After this point, the user is logged out. `flask_login.current_user.is_authenticated` is `False` and everything seems fine.

However, with every request, the user_loader is called with the old `user_id` which bears no meaning anymore. Also, at this point there is no need for flask_login to call `user_loader`.

I fixed this at this moment by setting the user_id session to `None`:

    @login_manager.user_loader
    def user_loader(id):
        user = SessionUser.find_by_session_id(id) #hits the database
        if user==None:
            flash('You have been logged out')
            session['user_id'] = None
        return user

However, I think this should be solved within flash_login itself. I think [reload_user](https://github.com/maxcountryman/flask-login/blob/41686db687a94a7a2eb2aa4b0abc3007b02bd54a/flask_login/login_manager.py#L305) should be changed, and have added the line `session['user_id'] = None`.

I'm happy to create a PR. Let me know what you think! I'm new to flask and I'm here to learn.

issue_title: `enabled=False` doesn't work for methods that access `response['id']`
issue_body:
Currently python-mailchimp contains code like this in various methods:

```python
class ListMembers(BaseApi):
    def create(self, list_id, data):
        # ...
        response = self._mc_client._post(url=self._build_path(list_id, 'members'), data=data)
        self.subscriber_hash = response['id']
        return response
```

Unfortunately, when `enabled=False`, this fails because `self._mc_client._post` returns `None`:
```
>       self.subscriber_hash = response['id']
E       TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable
```

I think `self.subscriber_hash = response['id'] if response else None` would do the job.

@stephenross If you added this code in your recent refactor perhaps you have an opinion on this? Thanks!

issue_title: Incompatibility with django-compressor 2.1
issue_body:
*django-compressor* released a [breaking change](https://github.com/django-compressor/django-compressor/commit/c0010bf2ae4064c11cfee2387f6e06c3880fc6d3#diff-01dc27e1db84256620f15d8a69b327caL119) to their interface from
`compressor.offline.django.DjangoParser.get_nodelist(self, node, original)`
to
`compressor.offline.django.DjangoParser.get_nodelist(self, node, original, context)`

so `sass_processor.management.commands.compilescss.Command.walk_nodes()` is now broken.

I see no easy way to make this change backward compatible with `django-compressor` <= 2.0 *and* Python 2, as the introspection API to get callable's signature was also changed. I suppose, the best course of actions is to ask `django-compressor` to make this `context` argument have a default value (`None`).

(I'm ended up with hard switching to compressor 2.1, as I no longer need 2.0 compatibility)

issue_title: figure out a way to reconcile swagger's limited variable types for non-body parameters.
issue_body:
The swagger spec specifically states that parameters that are not in body should adhere to a specific subset of types.

http://swagger.io/specification/#parameterObject

It's probably a good idea to deserialize / serialize object types to and from these basic types as necessary (using something like a string).

issue_title: frozensetlist.__hash__ should be order-sensitive
WIP: support for quadratic curves
issue_body:
There are some not implemented errors, mostly related to support for quadratic curves.

While splitting and merging existing curves there are three scenarios possible:
* the beginning of curve is needed
* the ending is needed
* a part somewhere in the middle is needed

A quadratic curves exists of one or more sub quadratic segments, in fontTools those can be created with `decomposeQaudraticSegment(...)`

Therefore the `inputContour.split(tValues)` have to split the quad curve nicely in parts, without adding points (by decomposing the curve).

This is work in progress, any help is welcome!

issue_title: Updating newly flushed object can break `changeset`
issue_body:
I've noticed that `changeset` method on versions returns empty dict in certain cases involving flushing. 

To reproduce:
 - Create new versioned object and add it to session
 - Flush session with the newly created object
 - Update the object
 - Commit the session

After that, asking for new object's `versions[0].changeset` returns empty dict, instead of all attributes set on the object, and version object has operation type of update, not insert (this is the main problem IMHO).

Here's a reduced example:
https://gist.github.com/oinopion/ac2c232a1777632a0d06d1fbe7018a7d

issue_title: Handling of / keeping data from migrations?
issue_body:
Sorry for my ignorance, but does this handle / keep / restore data being created during migrations?

The typical problem is that Django's `flush` (called with `TransactionTestcase`) will empty the tables, but keeps their structure migrated (see https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-django/pull/220#issuecomment-83310828 for more information / discussion in this regard), and migrations won't be run again (the `migrations` table/info is kept).

issue_title: Multiple range filters with non-unicode names don't work
issue_body:
Call to django _slugify_ function [here](https://github.com/silentsokolov/django-admin-rangefilter/blob/f034660dde7212b25bdd3853c0d11092807fcb6a/rangefilter/filter.py#L57) converts any non-unicode characters (e.g. cyrillic) to empty characters, which leads to all _system_name_ fields being empty. In this case, any date range filters except the first one don't work at all.

Suggested solution:
add `transliterate` package to requirements and instead of `from django.template.defaultfilters import slugify` use `from transliterate.utils import slugify`. That fixes the issue (cyrillic characters get transliterated correctly) and has no side effects as far as I see.

issue_title: Website header's "Support" link only works from the main page, not any "Docs" page

If you're on the [main page](https://facebook.github.io/watchman/) then clicking on the "Support" link up in the header will correctly bring you to: https://facebook.github.io/watchman/support.html

However, if you're within one of the ["Docs" pages](https://facebook.github.io/watchman/docs/install.html) then the relative link will be broken as it erroneously tries to send you to something like the following to 404 out: https://facebook.github.io/watchman/docs/support.html

It looks like the needs to be made absolute or more context aware.

    class TestClass(object):
        def __init__(self):
            self.attr2 = [1, 2, 3, 4]


    class TestJsonpickle(unittest.TestCase):
        def test_jsonpickle(self):
            import jsonpickle

            obj = TestClass()
            encoded = jsonpickle.encode(obj)
            robj = jsonpickle.decode(encoded)
            self.assertEqual(obj.__class__.__name__, robj.__class__.__name__)

The encoded string is

Running the uni test with `test/runtest.py` doesn't run the independently. Mock that doesn't clean themselves correctly after a test may cause other totally unrelated test to fail (c4905da). Running the test with nosetests doesn't create those side effect.

Currently `test/worker_test.py:MultipleWorkersTest.test_multiple_workers` fails under python3 because of those side effect. Renaming `worker_test.py` to `a_worker_test.py` magically "solves" the issue since `test_multiple_workers` is run before the culprit test.

issue_title: boto3 converts source code into Dockerfile on Beanstalk
issue_body:
We are using boto3 to create a new Elastic Beanstalk environment that runs on Docker. Unfortunately when we create the environment, the entire source code is converted into a Dockerfile. We are creating a new Beanstalk Application Version and setting `SourceBundle` as a zip file located on S3 before creating the new environment.

We do not experience this behavior when using the `awsebcli` so it seems like this is an issue with how boto is setting up the application version and/or creating the environment. I have included some details of the code for reference.

**Creating an Application Version**:

```
    source_code = f'archives/{version.commit}.zip'

    client.create_application_version(
        ApplicationName='Foobar App',
        VersionLabel=version.commit,
        Description=f"Commit: {version.description}",
        SourceBundle={
            'S3Bucket': 'mybucket',
            'S3Key': source_code
        },
        AutoCreateApplication=False,
        Process=True
    )
```

**Creating Elastic Beanstalk Environment**:

```
response = client.create_environment(
        ApplicationName='Foobar App',
        EnvironmentName=environment.name,
        Description=f'Feature Environment for {environment.name}',
        Tags=FEATURE_TAGS,
        VersionLabel=version.commit,
        TemplateName='webserver',
        OptionSettings=[
            {
                'Namespace': 'aws:elasticbeanstalk:application:environment',
                'OptionName': 'REELIO_PG_HOST',
                'Value': f'{environment.name}.xxxx.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com'
            },
            {
                'Namespace': 'aws:elasticbeanstalk:application:environment',
                'OptionName': 'REELIO_REDIS_HOST',
                'Value': f'{environment.name}.xxx.0001.use1.cache.amazonaws.com'
            },
        ]
    )
```
**Beanstalk Configuration Template**:

```
AWSConfigurationTemplateVersion: 1.1.0.0
EnvironmentConfigurationMetadata:
  DateCreated: '1488998001000'
  DateModified: '1501608721804'
  Description: Configuration created from the EB CLI using "eb config save".
EnvironmentTier:
  Name: WebServer
  Type: Standard
OptionSettings:
  aws:autoscaling:launchconfiguration:
    EC2KeyName: xxxxxx
    IamInstanceProfile: xxxxx
    InstanceType: t2.small
    RootVolumeSize: '25'
    RootVolumeType: standard
    SecurityGroups:
    - xxxxxx
  aws:autoscaling:updatepolicy:rollingupdate:
    MinInstancesInService: 1
    RollingUpdateEnabled: true
    RollingUpdateType: Health
  aws:elasticbeanstalk:application:environment:
    URL: staging.pro.reelio.com
  aws:elasticbeanstalk:command:

issue_title: Create enum for source field
issue_body:
The [source.yml](https://github.com/inspirehep/inspire-schemas/blob/master/inspire_schemas/records/elements/source.yml) is currently a free text field. This will be a problem when we start using this field value as the way to retrieve records from different sources (arXiv, APS, ...) by the merger, since this value will be in the database and should always be the same.

An enum should be created instead.

NOTE: One of the values in the enum should be used for the user submission forms. The forms currently don't populate the `source` field, but they should start doing that once we have the merger working.

For example:

```python
# This should be '00:1c:73:2a:60:62'
>>> import netaddr

>>> print netaddr.EUI(mac, dialect=netaddr.mac_unix_expanded)

 ```

I've confirmed that the `mac_address` field for `nsot.api.serializers.InterfaceSerializer` is a `CharField` and needs to be corrected.

issue_title: Command is terminated after 0.1 seconds when sudo_password is used
issue_body:
When calling `SSHLibrary.execute_command(command='sleep 5', sudo=True,  sudo_password='mypassword')` (or `SSHLibrary.start_command(...)`), the function closes the SSH shell if the command took longer than 0.1 seconds.

This is happening because of [this](https://github.com/robotframework/SSHLibrary/blob/master/src/SSHLibrary/pythonclient.py#L236) code:

    def _execute_with_sudo(self, sudo_password=None):
        ...
        if sudo_password is not None:
            stdin = self._shell.makefile('wb', -1)
            stdin.write(sudo_password + '\n')
            stdin.flush()
            time.sleep(0.1)  # flags from _shell_open() not updated without this sleep
            # in case of incorrect password close the shell
            if self._shell_open():  # This line causes execution termination.
                self._shell.close()

As I understand, the code assumes that if the command is not finished in 0.1 seconds after we entered the password, then the password is incorrect. This logic works for short-living (<0.1 seconds) commands, but fails for long-living ones.

I am not familiar with SSH protocol, so I don't know the general solution to this problem.
Can we peek stdout and check for the string "Sorry, try again." in case of incorrect password? Or have that string as a `SSHLibrary.DEFAULT_SUDO_WRONG_PASSORD_MESSAGE`?


Here is an example *\*.robot*:

    *** Settings ***
    Library  SSHLibrary
    
    *** Variables ***
    ${target_address}  10.42.0.2
    ${username}  <username>
    ${password}  <password>
    
    
    *** Test Cases ***
    
    Execute sudo command
      SSHLibrary.Open Connection  ${target_address}  prompt=$
      SSHLibrary.Login  ${username}  ${password}
    
      ${stdout}  ${stderr}=  SSHLibrary.Execute Command  sleep 5; echo end   return_stderr=True  sudo=True  sudo_password=${password}
      SSHLibrary.Close Connection

and output *log.html*:

    10:38:13.634 	INFO 	Executing command 'sudo sleep 5; echo end'.
    10:38:13.634 	DEBUG 	[chan 1] Max packet in: 32768 bytes
    10:38:13.741 	DEBUG 	[chan 1] EOF sent (1)
    10:38:13.742 	INFO 	Command exited with return code -1.
    10:38:13.742 	INFO 	${stdout} = <password>
    [sudo] password for <username>:

    10:38:13.742 	INFO 	${stderr} =

issue_title: Externally useful attributes should be declared public and documented.
issue_body:
Old Selenium2Library has several attributes that are useful externally from custom libraries and extensions:

```
_run_on_failure_keyword
_speed_in_secs
_timeout_in_secs
_implicit_wait_in_secs
_current_browser()
```

We should indicate that using these externally is OK by removing the leading underscore. The current WebDriver instance should also be accessible as a normal attribute (i.e. without calling a method like `current_browser()`) and we should name the attribute `webdriver` to make it explicit what it actually is. I would also remove unnecessary `_in_secs` postfixes. In other words, I would like to have these attributes instead:

```
run_on_failure_keyword
speed
timeout
implicit_wait
webdriver
```

Renaming these attributes is obviously a backwards incompatible change. We should thus add propertys that enable the old functionality but emit deprecation warnings. These propertys can then be removed in 3.1 or later.

---

*UPDATES:*

1. We used `browser` attribute instead of `webdriver` until 3.0 RC 1.

2. In 3.0 RC 1 we added also methods `find_element` and `find_elements` directly to the library itself. These methods form the recommended public API to find elements for external tools.

issue_title: The default function in HashingPartitioner is wrong
issue_body:
https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/2cb39da37356d3a73554325a663915698d4dbb97/pykafka/partitioners.py#L53-L61

Bad Python form. If you want anything resembling consistency, `hash` should never be used. There's no guarantee that it's going to produce consistent results between architectures and interpreter restarts. Instead, it should be using sha1 or, even better, mmh3, as the default.

issue_title: PSF photometry on crowded CCD frames
issue_body:
Hi there. I occasionally need to perform PSF photometry on crowded fields, and I'm looking to get rid of IRAF as soon as possible. This package seems to be ideal replacement, but for what I can gather `photutils` it is still in a heavy development phase.

Below are the steps needed to complete a full PSF photometry process, and the packages in IRAF used in each step (as described in [A User's Guide to Stellar CCD Photometry with IRAF](https://www.mn.uio.no/astro/english/services/it/help/visualization/iraf/daophot2.pdf)). I was wondering if these features are in the roadmap to be implemented, or if they are not planned at all.

1. Alignment of CCD images taken with different filters and exposure times (`imalign`)
1. Combine images with equivalent exposures to reduce S/N ratio (`imcombine`)
1. Standard stars aperture photometry (`daophot --> daofind, phot`)
1. Standard stars catalog to convert instrumental magnitudes to standard (`photcal --> mkcatalog, mknobsfile, mkconfig, fitparams`)
1. PSF photometry on all filters and exposures observed for the crowded field (`daophot --> daofind, phot, psf, nstar, substar, allstar`)
1. Transformation of stars' magnitudes in crowded field to standard system (`daophot --> mkobsfile, invertfit`)
1. Combine all exposures into a single observation for all stars in the field (no IRAF task as far as I am aware)

Sorry for the long post.

I've found a workaround: Override the static file `admin/js/related-widget-wrapper.js`  with a copy where you've added the following line:

issue_title: Document nupic.support
Use swig %pythonbegin instead of PREPEND_BOILERPLATE_TO_PYTHON_PROXY_MODULE
issue_body:
PR #1030 added [PREPEND_BOILERPLATE_TO_PYTHON_PROXY_MODULE](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/pull/1030/files#diff-95e351a3805a1dafa85bf20b81d086e6R715) to prepend the necessary boilerplate python code and file header from src/nupic/bindings/swig_proxy_preamble.py to the swig-generated python proxy modules.

I later learned that this can be accomplished via the Swig element [%pythonbegin](http://swig.org/Doc3.0/Python.html#Python_nn42) in the corresponding `.i` files instead of creating  custom commands during the build.

The idea is that the solution should include the shared src/nupic/bindings/swig_proxy_preamble.py contents, but not copy-paste them into the `.i` files. Copying manually to various `.i` files is bad for maintainability, so don't do it.

issue_title: upgrader: wrong result of latest_applied_upgrade
issue_body:
**Description**
I was trying to generate `upgrade script` by invoking:
```
inveniomanage upgrader create recipe -r zenodo -n new_subtype_proposal -p zenodo.base
```
what I discover is that `upgrader` produce wrong (not **the latest**) `depends_on` variable in this newly generated script.

What happens is that this query: [upgrader/engine.py#L323-L325](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/blob/pu/invenio/modules/upgrader/engine.py#L323-L325) gets the latest upgrade based on the `applied` property, which can lead to wrong result, eg. in my local env in `communities` module first 5 Upgrades has the same `applied` attribute:
```
2014-11-13 10:50:41
2014-11-13 10:50:41
2014-11-13 10:50:41
2014-11-13 10:50:41
2014-11-13 10:50:41
```
but looking at the `upgrade` property:
```
invenio_2012_12_11_new_citation_errors_table
invenio_2013_03_29_idxINDEX_stopwords_update
invenio_2013_03_28_idxINDEX_tokenizer
invenio_2013_03_25_idxINDEX_html_markup
invenio_2013_03_21_idxINDEX_stopwords
```
------------
**TL;DR**
I think that searching for the latest `Upgrade` by the `applied` property can result in getting not necessary the latest one - if by latest I mean that with the most recent creation date.

issue_title: Add Vagrant environment
RFC: Should the shredded JSON contain additional schema information?
issue_body:
The current JSON contexts shredding results in a simplified payload that results in a context name that includes the model along with the data e.g.,

```
    [
      ("context_com_acme_duplicated_1", [{"value": 1}, {"value": 2}]),
      ("context_com_acme_unduplicated_1", [{"unique": true}])
    ]
```
https://github.com/snowplow/snowplow-python-analytics-sdk/blob/master/snowplow_analytics_sdk/json_shredder.py#L102

This process is lossy and there are circumstances where the revision and addition components of the schemaver are important e.g., determining whether data is backwards compatible when running an aggregation, filtering/dropping on specific schema versions etc. Should the payload be restructured in include the schema version information (or more widely the schema information available to Redshift). Thoughts @chuwy ?

issue_title: Support *args and **kwargs in operation function signatures
issue_body:
### Description

This is a follow up to #268 to allow decorating operation functions where the decorator changes the signature to generic `*args`, `**kwargs`.

### Expected behaviour

Parameters should be mapped for operation functions with `*args` and/or `**kwargs`. 

### Actual behaviour

Parameters not directly matching the function signature are silently dropped (`kwargs` are not populated).

### Steps to reproduce

* Create a simple Swagger YAML with one operation having at least one query parameter "foo". 
* Define the operation function with only `*args` and `**kwargs`.
* The Swagger query parameter "foo" is not passed in `**kwargs`.

issue_title: Allow Basemap specification in df.carto_map
Add ASLR and DEP flags to linker where not on by default
issue_body:
Python 2.x distutils do not set the linker flags for ASLR and DEP by default, those are only set in Python 3.3+. And those flags are not on by default at least on VS 2008.

So to get ASLR add /DYNAMICBASE (or the 64-bit version with /HIGHENTROPYVA where supported). For DEP add /NXCOMPAT (but thats the default nearly anywhere).

Downside is, that mingw has problems with those two... :-(

issue_title: workspace: emit workspace files in standard, but human readable serialization format
issue_body:
Manticore's analysis results are emitted to a workspace directory, with a number of files for each generated state (Example below). Some of these files have an ad hoc format that is human readable, for example:

*.txt
```
STDIN_1: '\x01\x01\x01\x01\x00\x00\x00\xc0\x01\x01\
```

It's conceivable that users may want to process these output files with code, though. Currently, they need to write their own parsers. While not hard, they should not have to.

Standard serialization formats exist which are designed for human readability (e.g. http://yaml.org/). When appropriate Manticore, should formally emit its workspace files in one of these formats.

Specifically, I see no reason not to use yaml. It is quite human readable, stable, extensively used, and has wide support in programming languages.


---

```
-rw-r--r-- 1 501 dialout     44 Dec  1 13:28 command.sh
-rw-r--r-- 1 501 dialout   1238 Dec  1 13:28 test_00000000.messages
-rw-r--r-- 1 501 dialout      0 Dec  1 13:28 test_00000000.net
-rw-r--r-- 1 501 dialout 414563 Dec  1 13:28 test_00000000.pkl
-rw-r--r-- 1 501 dialout   2916 Dec  1 13:28 test_00000000.smt
-rw-r--r-- 1 501 dialout      0 Dec  1 13:28 test_00000000.stderr
-rw-r--r-- 1 501 dialout     12 Dec  1 13:28 test_00000000.stdin
-rw-r--r-- 1 501 dialout      0 Dec  1 13:28 test_00000000.stdout
-rw-r--r-- 1 501 dialout    927 Dec  1 13:28 test_00000000.syscalls
-rw-r--r-- 1 501 dialout  44032 Dec  1 13:28 test_00000000.trace
-rw-r--r-- 1 501 dialout   1036 Dec  1 13:28 test_00000000.txt
```

issue_title: Incorrect argument width being used with some syscalls
issue_body:
### Summary of the problem
For some system calls, Manticore uses the incorrect bitwidth for its arguments. More specifically, openat(2) interprets its first argument as a 64 bit signed number, where as it gets passed a 32 bit signed number.

### Step to reproduce the behavior

Emulate a binary that invokes openat() syscall, which is usually used for libc's `open()` implementation, especially when opening files in the cwd. Note the value of fd that gets passed to `sys_openat`.


### Any relevant logs

Example syscall trace:

```
2018-06-21 15:19:43,302: [42011] m.p.platform:DEBUG: sys_access(0x4a52fa (/etc/ld.so.nohwcap), 0x0) -> -1
2018-06-21 15:19:45,565: [42011] m.p.platform:DEBUG: sys_newuname(0x7ffffffffc10 (Linux)) -> 0
2018-06-21 15:19:45,892: [42011] m.p.platform:DEBUG: sys_newfstat(0x1, 0x7ffffffff3c0) -> 0
2018-06-21 15:19:45,935: [42011] m.p.platform:DEBUG: sys_ioctl(0x1, 0x5401, 0x7ffffffff320) -> -22
2018-06-21 15:19:47,445: [42011] m.p.platform:DEBUG: sys_openat(0xffffff9c, 0x4926cc (LICENSE), 0x0, 0x0) -> -9
2018-06-21 15:19:47,469: [42011] m.p.linux:INFO: Calling fd: 4294967295
2018-06-21 15:19:47,469: [42011] m.p.linux:INFO: Calling newfstat with invalid fd
2018-06-21 15:19:47,470: [42011] m.p.platform:DEBUG: sys_newfstat(0xffffffff, 0x7ffffffffb80) -> -9
```

Note that sys_openat() gets called with 0xffffff9c, and not -100 as it should be.

issue_title: Broadcasting sum
issue_body:
Is there a way of adding (column-wise) a column vector to a matrix?
I grouped a series of possible actions my system can take as columns in the E matrix. W is the weight matrix, b is the bias vector, and v is a row vector.
```
repr = dy.tanh(W*E+b)
dy.pickneglogsoftmax(v*repr, true_answer)
```

However I get an error trying to add the b vector to the W*E matrix. Is there a way to broadcast it?This is a single example from the training dataset, so I'd prefer to avoid minibatching functions until I am actually doing minibatching.

issue_title: `makeotf` option description is not accurate (`-cs` / `-cl`)
issue_body:
option `-cs` / `-cl` described as follows:

> Override the heuristics, and specify the ScriptID / LanguageID for the Macintosh 'cmap' subtable; CID-keyed fonts only.

By my test, the option will determine the value of `platEncID` / `language` for `cmap_format_6`, this not only affects the Mac platform, **and the option is also valid for name-keyed fonts**.

issue_title: Add preemptive authentication
issue_body: I'd like to use opportunistic authentication on the server end and simply check for WWW-Authenticate header presence. The main idea is to prevent the first HTTP request which will always return 401 and instead supply the Kerberos credentials immideately. At first glance it seems to be pretty easy to implement on the client side.

issue_title: BigQuery: get_job() and list_jobs() fail when there has been an invalid job request
[Feature Request] Avoid pickling object attributes matching user-defined predicates
issue_body:
Greetings from the still-frigid Great White North (i.e., _Canada_). Upfront apologies for polluting your issue tracker with yet another frivolous feature request: in this case, **global attribute filtering.**

## What U Want?

Our [downstream application](https://gitlab.com/betse/betse) would *love* to avoid pickling any object attributes whose attribute names match a user-defined predicate – regardless of object type. Sadly, neither the underlying `pickle` API nor the higher-level `dill` API appear to be extensible in this way. Both permit callers to define custom reduction functions uniquely applicable to *specific* types but appear to have no means of defining custom reduction functions globally applicable to *all* types. 

## Why U Want This?

A bit of context would probably be helpful. Like most ~~obese~~ large-scale Python projects, ours provides a `@property`-like decorator (named [`@property_cached`](https://gitlab.com/betse/betse/blob/master/betse/util/type/call/memoizers.py)) efficiently caching property return values into object attributes whose names are *all* prefixed by the magic substring `__property_cached_`. Efficiency is fun, so this works great!

**Except under pickling.**

These attributes tend to be quite large in worst-case scenarios – like several gigabytes too large. Since these attributes are all safely recomputable at runtime, we'd rather *not* pickle them if we don't have to. Since any arbitrary object of any arbitrary type may contain methods decorated by `@property_cached` and hence these attributes, omitting these attributes from pickling with one or more custom reduction functions specific to particular types isn't feasible.

A general-purpose strategy is needed.

## Why Don't U Do This the Easy Way?

"It'll be easy!", I initially said. It wasn't easy. After several hours of futile grepping through the `pickle` and `dill` codebases, I'm no closer to a sane solution. At this point, I'll happily accept an insane solution.

The only one on hand appears to be:

* Define a new application specific `SmartPickleableMixin` class implementing the `__getstate__()` special method to omit all object attributes prefixed by the magic substring `__property_cached_`.
* Refactor *all* existing application classes to additionally subclass the `SmartPickleableMixin` class.

This is pretty insane for any number of obvious reasons, the least of which is that implementing this change across a codebase exceeding 100,000 lines of raw, brutal, no-holds-barred scientific Python will probably require two human lifetimes. Moreover, it's manual and hence fragile – really, *really* fragile.

Humans are imperfect meat bags. Any class that I or other project contributors accidentally neglect to subclass from `SmartPickleableMixin` will continue to silently pickle cached properties – which is bad.

Hence, this modest request for a global attribute filtering solution applicable to *all* types.

## This Issue Too Long

Right. Sorry about that.

**In summary:** `dill` is simply amazing. It's single-handedly made our scientific use case feasible. Whether this feature ever comes to fruition or not, thanks for this laborious work of gritty, low-level volunteerism.

Springtime pineapples for all! :pineapple: :pineapple: :pineapple:

issue_title: New column format is not DRY
issue_body:
Going through the code, I realized this column format is deprecated:

    class MyDatatable(Datatable):
        class Meta:
            columns = [
                ("ID", 'id', helpers.link_to_model),
                ("Blog", 'blog__name', helpers.link_to_model(key=lambda instance: instance.blog)),
                ("Headline", 'headline', helpers.make_xeditable),
                ("Body Text", 'body_text', helpers.itemgetter(slice(0, 30))),
                ("Publication Date", 'pub_date', helpers.format_date('%A, %b %d, %Y')),
                ("Modified Date", 'mod_date'),
                ("Age", 'pub_date', helpers.through_filter(timesince)),
                ("Interaction", 'get_interaction_total', helpers.make_boolean_checkmark),
                ("Comments", 'n_comments', helpers.format("{0:,}")),
                ("Pingbacks", 'n_pingbacks', helpers.format("{0:,}")),
            ]

in favor of:

    class MyDatatable(Datatable):
        class Meta:
            columns = ['id', 'blog_name', 'headline', 'body_text', 'pub_date', 'mod_date', 'age',
                       'interaction', 'n_comments', 'n_pingbacks']
            processors = {
                'id': helpers.link_to_model,
                'blog_name': helpers.link_to_model(key=lambda obj: obj.blog),
                'headline': helpers.make_xeditable,
                'body_text': helpers.itemgetter(slice(0, 30)),
                'pub_date': helpers.format_date('%A, %b %d, %Y'),
                'n_comments': helpers.format("{0:,}"),
                'n_pingbacks': helpers.format("{0:,}"),
            }

I like the older format better not only because it is more DRY (you don't have to repeat field names here and there, but also you have all the settings for a field in one place, which makes code easier to read. 

It is also possible to have all settings of a column/field in one place by explicitly defining it in the form of: `blog_name = columns.TextColumn(...` but that is verbose.

issue_title: Allow the ability to ask for universal distributions
issue_body: Right now the Translator takes platform/interpreter keyword arguments.  If they're None, we default to the current platform and current interpreter respectively.  However, the desire may be to *ensure* that we pick a platform-inspecific version of a wheel (e.g. thrift can be built w/ or w/o C extensions and we want a way to require the former.)  This may mean changing the semantics of Translator such that interpreter=None means "py2.py3" and platform=None means "none-any".  (This of course only works for wheels.)

issue_title: Scalafmt update
issue_body:
There were some issues raised during review that were marked Done but appear to be still live. @stuhood intends to take another pass so this is to track them.

- It is skip=False by default
       * Scala consumers first interaction with Pants will likely be an error that asks to reformat their source code.
-  Operates over every Target in self.context.targets().
       * Should limit itself to scala_library and tests.
- Uses goal based scheduling instead of using Products.
       * The SimpleCodegen products model is probably a more flexible choice.
       * What if someone wants another compile task to go first?
        * I don't think scalafmt warrants hardcoded priority, it doesn't actually need anything besides to run before `compile.zinc`
- There is a default config file set in the task, but I do not believe that config file is being made available in the sdist.
- Upon failure, it prints the full (adjusted?) text of one file to terminal
- It suggests running

        `FAILURE: Scalafmt failed with exit code 899 to fix run: `scalafmt -i --files ${files}`

where the `${files[@]}` are every scala file it ran over, whether it passed or not.

* There is no config file included in the suggested output, so the "fix" run may use a different config than the check config.

- It bootstraps its own scalafmt tool, but suggests using the system scalafmt.
       * It could at least point at the bootstrapped tool (and include the path to the config file).
       * Our engineers do not currently have a system `scalafmt`, for example
- A nice-to-have would be to surface the scalafmt tool as an option, like the test.junit does for the runner.

issue_title: [Feature Request] Support exporting the model in ONNX model format
issue_body:
Currently, users train the model with Keras-MXNet, save the model as MXNet model using save_mxnet_model() API and use MXNet engine for inference.

However, if we can support a new API - save_onnx_model(), users can train with Keras-MXNet and export the model as ONNX model, and use different ONNX toolchain for inference.

High level implementation details:
1. Update mxnet.contrib.onnx.export_model() to accept symbol and params or module object as the input. (current it expects the symbol and params file).
2. Add new API in in keras/engine/saving.py - save_onnx_model() -> That calls internals of save_mxnet_model() to fetch MXNet native model details, calls mxnet.contrib.onnx.export_model() and saves the model as onnx.

Note:
For this, we might require protobuf and other dynamic dependency for onnx. Need to dive deep on how we can handle this.

issue_title: Modifying Existing Resources - Adding APIs, Event Sources etc
issue_body:
Parent tracking Issue for a large class of questions - "Can SAM modify existing resources?". More specifically:

- Can SAM add API path to an existing API Gateway resource? (#232)
- Can SAM add trigger to an existing S3 bucket? (#124)
- Can SAM add trigger to existing IoT Topic Rule? (#241)

The answer for most of them is NO. SAM templates are deployed using AWS CloudFormation which does not support modifying existing resources created "outside" of this CloudFormation stack. For example, adding S3 trigger to an existing bucket will require SAM to add a `NotificationConfiguration` property to the bucket. Since CloudFormation can't modify resources created outside of stack, this is a no go with SAM. If your requirement falls under this umbrella, then it is not possible to support it in SAM today.

Technically speaking, this behavior is out of bounds for native CloudFormation resources, but we can always implement "custom resources" which do this for you. Custom Resources are Lambda functions that can make relevant API calls without being limited by CloudFormation's native constraints. 

Keeping this tracking issue open to collect all requirements for modifying existing resources. If this gathers enough steam, SAM can automatically create and maintain these custom resources to do the job for you.

issue_title: On data structures and DIDL Lite for music services
issue_body:
Hi everyone

At this point I have commented quite a few places about what you need, to create a data structure (and the DIDL metadata necessary to be able to play it) for a music service, so I thought it would be better to gather this in one place, so maybe we can clear some things up.

Here is an example from two different music services of both the XML sent to the Sonos unit to add a track to the queue and the information that you receive from the music service server when you ask for the track:

```xml
<!-- Wimp -->

<!-- ### Sent to the player when adding a track -->
<!-- BODY -->
<s:Envelope xmlns:s="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" s:encodingStyle="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/encoding/">
  <s:Body>
    <u:AddURIToQueue xmlns:u="urn:schemas-upnp-org:service:AVTransport:1">
      <InstanceID>0</InstanceID>
      <EnqueuedURI>x-sonos-http:trackid_22757082.mp4?sid=20&flags=32</EnqueuedURI>
      <EnqueuedURIMetaData>DIDL_REPLACEMENT_0</EnqueuedURIMetaData>
      <DesiredFirstTrackNumberEnqueued>0</DesiredFirstTrackNumberEnqueued>
      <EnqueueAsNext>0</EnqueueAsNext>
    </u:AddURIToQueue>
  </s:Body>
</s:Envelope>

<DIDL-Lite xmlns:dc="http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/" xmlns:upnp="urn:schemas-upnp-org:metadata-1-0/upnp/" xmlns:r="urn:schemas-rinconnetworks-com:metadata-1-0/" xmlns="urn:schemas-upnp-org:metadata-1-0/DIDL-Lite/">
  <item id="00030020trackid_22757082" parentID="0004002calbumid_22757081" restricted="true">
    <dc:title>Chord Left</dc:title>
    <upnp:class>object.item.audioItem.musicTrack</upnp:class>
    <desc id="cdudn" nameSpace="urn:schemas-rinconnetworks-com:metadata-1-0/">SA_RINCON5127_42????35</desc>

  <ns0:mimeType>audio/aac</ns0:mimeType>
  <ns0:title>Superwoman (Radio Edit)</ns0:title>
</ns0:mediaMetadata>
```

From this, there is a few things to notice:
 * While we still need to send DIDL to the Sonos unit, what we get from the music service server is not. The name space is `http://www.sonos.com/Services/1.1`, which I guess, if we are unlucky, could mean that this is Sonos own spec and maybe you can only get you hands on it, if you are a music service.
 * In the DIDL metadata, note that the content of the `desc` tag is not longer constant, in fact it includes my username for the service (I have added a few ??? to the Wimp one, as the username there is my phone number). Also note, that this information is not contained in the XML we get back. This means (as far as I can tell) that a pure from-XML method will not be possible, which is the reason that the `from_xml` method on the music service structures takes the music service instance as an argument, so that it can ask the plugin for this information.
 * The XML from the music service server also (I guess because it is not in fact an DIDL structure) does not contain a DIDL class, only an itemType. This means that the usual way of figuring out which music service structure should be used by looking at the class (and hence collapsing several types with the same class into one) will not work. I can of course see that class with Wireshark, if I add the item to the queue, but since not all elements can be added to the queue, there are some elements that I do not know the DIDL class for (Note, that you can stil browse them, for that you need only to send the ID to the music service server). But of course, one might also argue, that if it is only turned into a DIDL class to add it to the queue, that the question is, if it really makes sense to talk about a DIDL class for items that cannot be enqueued and that of course re-raises the question about whether the music service structure should have their own hierarchy. The only hope with regards to detecting the DIDL class that I can think if, is if it can be inferred somehow, see below.
 * The XML from the music service does contains an ID, but it is not the complete ID used when sending the item to the Sonos unit - a 8 digit prefix is added. Initially I though this was just an indication of the class, but as you can see, they do not match for these two tracks, which have the same DIDL class. It could also be a kind code to permission e.g. the `canPlay`, `canBrowse` fields in the music service XML (or a combination of the two), but so far, I have been unable to detect a pattern. The prefixes I have been able to gather so far are the following:
```python
# Wimp
ID_PREFIX = {
    MSTrack: '00030020',
    MSAlbum: '0004002c',
    MSArtist: '10050024',
    MSAlbumList: '000d006c',
    MSPlaylist: '0006006c',
    MSArtistTracklist: '100f006c',
    MSFavorites: None,  # This one is unknown
    MSCollection: None  # This one is unknown
}
ID_PREFIX = {
    MSTrack: '00030020',
    MSAlbum: '0004006c',
    MSArtist: '10050024',
    MSAlbumList: '000d006c',
    MSPlaylist: '0006006c',
    MSArtistTracklist: '100f006c',
    MSContainer: '1008006c',
    MSTracklist: '000e006c',
}
```
and so this constitutes the second reason that we need to give the from_xml the plugin instance, it needs to ask the plugin what the prefix is.

Another related problem is, that as far as I remember (I'm really not sure here), we also do not have all the information that we need to re-add the item, when we get information about a music service item back from Sonos unit (like e.g. when asking for the queue #234):
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" ?>
<ns0:item id="Q:0/21" parentID="Q:0" restricted="true" xmlns:dc="http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/" xmlns:ns0="urn:schemas-upnp-org:metadata-1-0/DIDL-Lite/" xmlns:ns1="urn:schemas-upnp-org:metadata-1-0/upnp/">
  <ns0:res duration="0:04:09" protocolInfo="sonos.com-http:*:audio/mp4:*">x-sonos-http:trackid_32823104.mp4?sid=20&flags=32</ns0:res>
  <ns1:albumArtURI>/getaa?s=1&u=x-sonos-http%3atrackid_32823104.mp4%3fsid%3d20%26flags%3d32</ns1:albumArtURI>
  <dc:title>I Want You</dc:title>
  <ns1:class>object.item.audioItem.musicTrack</ns1:class>
  <dc:creator>Tina Dickow</dc:creator>
  <ns1:album>Whispers</ns1:album>
</ns0:item>
```
(We are missing the content for the `desc` field, which includes my music service user name and the ID prefix). This essentially mean that, if it should be possibly to re-add queue items, then it will be necessary to provide the SoCo instance with a complete list of relevant plugin instances (that the queue elements come from), in order for it to be able to qualify them properly.

All right. I very much look forward to peoples insights here as there is still quite a bit of manual work left for creating a new MS plugin and I really hope there is a shortcut somewhere. Of course, if @lawrenceakka manages to find the Sonos music service plugin spec (I could not), then just ignore everything I just wrote ;)

Regards (from shoulder-burping and diaper-changing land)
Kenneth

EDITED: 2014-10-21 Clarify a few things.

issue_title: Incorrect call of HTMLUnicodeInputStream in _inputstream.py
issue_body:
Hi, while using Weasyprint 0.29 (that uses html5lib 0.999999999) for printing templates, I have found following issue:

`env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/html5lib/_inputstream.py", line 149, in HTMLInputStream
    return HTMLUnicodeInputStream(source, **kwargs)
TypeError: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'encoding'`

It seems that it is caused by changes in issue #120 (line 149). **HTMLUnicodeInputStream** constructor is called with multiple arguments, but the constructor itself has only one parameter.

Edit: version **0.9999999** (7x9) is working correctly.

issue_title: class_hash only iterates on direct ancestor classes while should iterate over mro
issue_body:
If you have a special representation for 
```
class A:
  pass
```
then it works for 
```
class B(A):
  pass
```
as well but not for
```
class C(B):
  pass
```

To fix this you need this line https://github.com/cognitect/transit-python/blob/master/transit/class_hash.py#L30 changed to 
```
for t in key.mro():
```

issue_title: Should string parameters be allowed in certain functions of the Factor class?
issue_body:
Till now functions like get_cardinality, reduce, maximize and marginalize (in the factor class) do not take string parameters as input and throw a ValueError since it can take only type list or array-like parameters. I am currently working to display a proper TypeError message for such cases.
But now, I think string parameters should also be allowed. We can check for the type of the parameter and if being a type str we can convert it into a type list within the function itself.

issue_title: Deis doesn't actually stop containers upon scale down & destroy
issue_body:
Hi,

I didn't take the time to document anything meticulously, but here's the story:

- had an app on deis with ~10 web containers, ~3 celery workers
- decided to migrate the app off of Deis
- scaled all processes down to 0 containers
- ran `deis destroy -a my_app`

Deis reported everything as destroyed and scaled down, app is no longer accessible via HTTP.

But. 

Emails from the app keep rolling in with the deis fqdn. Apparently Celery is still processing tasks on behalf of the deis app. I log into a deis node and, indeed, not only does `docker ps` reveal a still-running container, but when I kill the container it respawns. Eventually I tracked down the systemd conf file for the service which, to my dismay, was still loaded in the systemd daemon.

In order to *actually* stop my app on deis, I had to log into each host and
- remove the related systemd `.service` file
- `sudo systemctl daemon-reload`
- and finally `rm` all matching containers.

:sob:

issue_title: Error in docs: Revoke Token is more finicky than indicated
issue_body:
The docstring for `AuthClient.oauth2_revoke_token` indicates, in its examples section, that you can do revocations with bad creds or wrong creds.
I think this gives a bad impression, since apparently the endpoint is more restrictive than that.

Although I maintain that this should be fixed to be highly permissive server-side, we should meanwhile update the docstring to be accurate.

I'm not bothering about this for `0.4.2`, however.

issue_title: Old error in the SBCharSetProber.cpp (or .py) of the  Universal Charset Detector
issue_body:
Hello,

when I was working on my new language models for Central European countries I found old error in the sbcharsetprober.py (or .cpp) file.

I've looked around on the internet and I've found only ONE developer/contributor (PyYoshi) :  https://github.com/PyYoshi/cChardet/blob/af39b2f0fbd558422b46251fd927c266b2e4e721/src/ext/libcharsetdetect/mozilla/extensions/universalchardet/src/base/nsSBCharSetProber.cpp who corrected this amazing error (There are some other bugs are corrected and  added many new language models). 

In the code of all forks I've found (python, cpp, ...)  "-1" is missing (The part of source code below is already corrected):

```cpp
// Order is in [1-64] but we want 0-63 here.
order = mModel->charToOrderMap[(unsigned char)aBuf[i]] -1

if (order < SYMBOL_CAT_ORDER)
  mTotalChar++;
if (order < SAMPLE_SIZE)
  {
```

I've spent half a day to understand why my new lang models give very low
confidence value for tested text. With adding "-1" confident values are
normal.

If you can, please, post this info to other chardet developers.

Many thanks.
